perturbation.original_text: You will adhere to the Exam Supervision Guidelines and Exam Eligibility Guidelines as published from time to time by The Open Group at the certification site for the applicable program.
perturbation.changed_text: You may, at your discretion, adhere to the Exam Supervision Guidelines and Exam Eligibility Guidelines as published from time to time by The Open Group at the certification site for the applicable program.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the proctor 'will' adhere to the guidelines, creating a mandatory obligation. The modified text replaces 'will' with 'may, at your discretion', making adherence optional. This contradicts the intent of the agreement, which is to ensure the integrity of the exams. This change directly contradicts the later clause emphasizing the importance of the Exam Materials' integrity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Open Group spends substantial sums of time and money in developing and administering its Exam Materials and carefully guards their integrity and confidentiality;
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates adherence to Exam Supervision Guidelines, whereas the changed text makes adherence optional ('may, at your discretion'). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of maintaining the integrity and confidentiality of the Exam Materials. By making adherence optional, the changed text undermines the Open Group's efforts to protect the integrity of its exams, thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: You may not disclose the Exam questions or answers or discuss any of the content of the Exam Materials with any person, without prior written approval of The Open Group;
perturbation.changed_text: You may disclose the Exam questions or answers or discuss any of the content of the Exam Materials with any person, without prior written approval of The Open Group; because it may prove useful for marketing purposes
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits the disclosure of exam materials without prior approval. The modified text permits disclosure of exam materials without approval for marketing, directly contradicting the earlier prohibition. This creates uncertainty about what constitutes a violation of the agreement, especially given that the Open Group is said to guard their integrity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You may disclose the Exam questions or answers or discuss any of the content of the Exam Materials with any person, without prior written approval of The Open Group; because it may prove useful for marketing purposes
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits disclosing exam materials without prior written approval. The changed text permits disclosing them without approval for marketing purposes. This creates a direct conflict with the original restriction, thus satisfying the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The changed text and contradicted text are identical, which makes the contradiction even more evident.

perturbation.original_text: You will not copy or attempt to make copies (written, photocopied, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers;
perturbation.changed_text: You may make copies (written, photocopied, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers; for personal use only.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly forbids copying any exam material. The modified text allows copying for 'personal use only', directly contradicting the earlier prohibition. This introduces ambiguity and undermines the Open Group's intellectual property rights. The permission to copy creates an in-text contradiction with the initial restriction, where it says about protection of Intellectual Property Rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The questions and answers of the Exam are the exclusive and confidential property of The Open Group and are protected by The Open Group's intellectual property rights;
perturbation.justification: The original text forbade copying exam materials. The changed text permits copying for 'personal use only'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the exam questions and answers are the exclusive and confidential property of The Open Group and are protected by their intellectual property rights. Allowing any form of copying, even for personal use, undermines this protection and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You will not sell, license, distribute, give away, or obtain from any other source other Open Group Exam materials, questions or answers;
perturbation.changed_text: You may obtain from any other source other Open Group Exam materials, questions or answers; if they are available freely online
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits obtaining exam materials from any other source. The modified text permits obtaining materials if they are 'available freely online,' directly contradicting the earlier prohibition. This creates uncertainty about the legitimacy of exam preparation and undermines the integrity of the examination process. The fact that Open Group spends substantial sums of time and money in developing and administering its Exam Materials, means that they are important. Allowing for free availability will be in contradiction to guarding integrity and confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Open Group spends substantial sums of time and money in developing and administering its Exam Materials and carefully guards their integrity and confidentiality;
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits obtaining Open Group Exam materials from any source other than official channels. The changed text permits obtaining these materials if they are 'available freely online.' This directly contradicts the original prohibition and undermines the 'integrity and confidentiality' that Open Group aims to carefully guard, as stated in the 'Contradicted Text'. Therefore, the modified text creates a clear and direct conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of any of these provisions will cause irreparable harm to The Open Group for which monetary remedies may be inadequate, and that The Open Group shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to take all appropriate actions to remedy or prevent such disclosure or misuse, including obtaining an immediate injunction;
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of any of these provisions may, at The Open Group's discretion, cause irreparable harm to The Open Group for which monetary remedies may be inadequate, and that The Open Group shall be entitled, while waiving other rights or remedies, to take actions to remedy or prevent such disclosure or misuse, including obtaining an immediate injunction; limited to only a warning.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that violating any provision 'will' cause irreparable harm and that the Open Group 'shall' be entitled to take appropriate actions. The modified text changes 'will' to 'may, at The Open Group's discretion,' making the harm and the response uncertain. It also changes 'shall' to 'shall while waiving other rights or remedies' and adds 'limited to only a warning', contradicting the commitment to take 'all appropriate actions.' This weakens the enforcement mechanism of the agreement and it now contradicts itself. The original text is about taking all appropriate actions and the change is taking only limited action, which is a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violation of any of these provisions may, at The Open Group's discretion, cause irreparable harm to The Open Group for which monetary remedies may be inadequate, and that The Open Group shall be entitled, while waiving other rights or remedies, to take actions to remedy or prevent such disclosure or misuse, including obtaining an immediate injunction; limited to only a warning.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction. The original text mandates specific actions for violations ('will cause irreparable harm' and 'shall be entitled to take all appropriate actions'). The changed text weakens this by making the harm discretionary ('may, at The Open Group's discretion, cause irreparable harm') and limiting the response to 'only a warning' while waiving other rights. Since the contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed Text, the contradiction is that the Open Group now has the discretion to take no action beyond a warning, which directly contradicts the implied obligation to take 'all appropriate actions' as stated in the original text. Therefore the change creates an in-text contradiction.
